programme.title: Pressure's On
programme.desc: Using a multi-cooker, charming host Vanessa Gianfrancesco cooks up a four dishes that are salty, sweet, and surprising.

programme.title: Bonacini's Italy
programme.desc: Rice Balls Stuffed with Seafood and Pistachio Cannoli are on the menu as Chef Michael Bonacini explores dishes from the Italian island of Sicily.

programme.title: Bonacini's Italy
programme.desc: Piemonte cuisine is influenced by its mountainous terrain. Chef Michael Bonacini shows us how to make its warm regional dishes like Bagna Cauda.

programme.title: Up the Dish
programme.desc: Host Tiera Singh goes on a South American tour with Peruvian Ceviche. Marinated citrusy seabass and shrimp swim in a sea of sweet potatoes and creamy avocado - it's hard to beat! But Poppy Sandler takes a shot, and lands on Grilled Shrimp with a smoky charred Corn and Avocado salad, topped with garlic and lemon grilled jumbo shrimp. Next, Missy Hui indulges her sweet tooth with Vegan Key Lime Tarts. Her trick? Using avocado for extra cream and extra green. Branzino with Shishito and Mango is how Micah Trainor scales it up - her whole-fish-dish is pan-roasted and spiced with chaat masala, topped with mangoes and blistered shishito peppers for a five-star feast!

programme.title: Up the Dish
programme.desc: How do you make any lasagna recipe better? Ask Tiera Singh to take it up, of course! Her 'Nonna-style' bechamel and Bolognese-based Lasagna is topped with melty mozzarella for a luscious and luxuriant dish. Tiera tosses it over to Poppy Sandler, whose sideways Seafood Lasagna mixes up mussels, shrimp, and a briny bechamel sauce - yum! Next, Micah Trainor makes a dressed-up vegetarian version of the classic - her Zucchini-Mushroom Lasagna Rolls with tangy sundried tomatoes, savoury ricotta cheese, and earthy mushroom duxelles is simply superlative. Missy Hui hits the sweet spot with her Chocolate Raspberry Dessert Lasagna; layers of rich white chocolate, thick raspberry coulis, and velvety mascarpone cheese come together for a dessert that's in the stratosphere!

programme.title: Up the Dish
programme.desc: In this episode of Up the Dish, host Missy Hui goes back to her childhood with Chicken and Dumplings - comfort food for every age! The chicken-dumpling journey doesn't end there; Micah Trainor turns the dish upside down and inside out with her Chinese-inspired Chicken-Chive Gyoza, a crunchy-crispy dim sum delight, with an ovERS-the-top chili sauce! How does Poppy Sandler spice things up? Herb Gnudi with Red Pepper Sauce, a spicy-savoury dumpling that's coated in nutty brown butter and creamy ricotta cheese for extra oomph. Tiera Singh finishes with another European dish - Spinach Spaetzle and Crispy Prosciutto, straight from Germany! Chewy spaetzle, fresh spinach, crispy ham - this textural treat doesn't let up!

programme.title: Up the Dish
programme.desc: Host Tiera Singh gets the show on the road with her Italian- inspired Fennel Orange Salad. Seared sushi-grade tuna rests on top of a crunchy, zesty, tangy fennel and orange salad with a honey-vinegar dressing to sweeten it up! Micah Trainor's revamp? Five Spice Duck a L'Orange with a sugary caramelized orange sauce and duck fat fried potatoes - is it French? Chinese? Who knows, but it's delicious! Next, Poppy Sandler pulls off a vegetarian variation, with a Roasted Veggie Bouillabaisse, complete with musky morels, umami-packed kombu, and tender asparagus. Missy Hui finishes up with her Orange Licorice Panna Cotta - aromatic orange blossom honey and licorice-like fennel pollen make the dish a decadent denouement to this episode of Up the Dish!

programme.title: Up the Dish
programme.desc: Crack open a mussel or two, this episode of Up the Dish begins in Belgium, but please, no waffles! Host Micah Trainor makes Moules- Frites, mussels steamed in white wine and shows her trick for bistro- style French fries. Next, Tiera Singh steals the show with her Mussel Broth Vichyssoise and Smoked Mussels, an ultra-creamy cold soup that's paired with smoky shellfish for fantastic flavour. Vegetarian mussels? Missy Hui says "no worries!" and makes Saffron Artichokes with zesty Lemon Chili Oil - there's a little bit of everything in each delectable bite! How does Poppy Sandler send the episode off? By turning it on its head! Fried mussels with Celeriac Remoulade salad makes for a satisfying ending to Up the Dish.

programme.title: Up the Dish
programme.desc: In this episode of Up the Dish, steal away to Spain for a summer vacation as host Tiera Singh takes a swing at Paella, complete with sunny saffron, saucy sofrito, and super crunchy soccarat. Next, Missy Hui takes a break for brunch with Chorizo Eggs Benedict - the Saffron Hollandaise is good as gold and twice as bright! Poppy Sandler follows with her Fritto Misto and Sofrito, a delicate and fresh veggie fry-up that soaks up a tomato-spiked sofrito dipping sauce. Micah Trainor finishes up with restaurant-ready Razor Clams steamed in sparkling Spanish wine, served alongside nutty Iberico Ham and sweet Manchego cheese.

programme.title: Cook the Books
programme.desc: Chef-host Shahir Massoud tries out recipes from another award-winning cookbook: Anna Olson’s Baking Wisdom! Follow along as Shahir makes a breakfast galette, a classic apple tart, a rich brown butter pecan pie, and much more!

programme.title: Bonacini's Italy
programme.desc: Emilia Romagna is the culinary heart of Italy and Chef Michael Bonacini shows viewers how to make some of the region's most delicious dishes.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Kids don't always love fish - host Spencer Watts shares some simple, inexpensive, and tasty recipes that will change your kids' opinion of fish night.

programme.title: Cook Like a Chef
programme.desc: With ease and grace Chef Tim McRoberts bakes like a chef. Chef makes gorgeous chocolate molten cake, and demonstrates that art of working fondant.

programme.title: A is for Apple
programme.desc: Cheese or chocolate? Why not both! In this "C" challenge, host Theresa Visintin takes on both decadent ingredients. But first, she shows us that being cheesy is easy, especially when you stuff chicken breast with creamy goat's cheese and finely diced apples. Theresa takes the flavour one step further by coating the stuffed chicken with sage, parmesan, and panko bread crumbs, then frying it to a crispy golden brown. Next, Theresa whips up a dessert fan favourite, the decadent and oh-so-irresistible dark chocolate lava cake. Finally, Theresa has an irresistible recipe using ooey gooey cheese and warm chocolate.

programme.title: Bonacini's Italy
programme.desc: Simplicity is key with Sicilian cuisine and Chef Michael Bonacini explores just how the region balances simplicity with intensity through four flavourful dishes. Starting with an antipasto of Folded Flatbread with Ricotta and Fennel Seeds, Michael inspires with its intricate beauty with a simple recipe. Keeping to the theme of simplicity, Michael shows that plenty of flavour can come from little ingredients in his primo of Broccoli and Sausage Rigatoni. The spicy sausage and chili is countered by a creamy broccoli pesto for a dish that highlights Sicilian cuisine. Michael turns to the Italian queen of crops for his secondo: Eggplant Parmesan Roll-Ups. With thin slices of eggplant rolled up to contain a mouth-watering mixture of mozzarella, provolone, and pecorino romano, this dish gets an extra kick of flavour from a coating of breadcrumbs and tomato sauce. To bring it all together, Michael combines spicy with sweet through his tender Spiced Chocolate Cookies.

programme.title: Pressure's On
programme.desc: Using a multi-cooker, charming host Vanessa Gianfrancesco cooks up a four dishes that are salty, sweet, and surprising.

programme.title: Griddle Nation
programme.desc: Spice lovers rejoice as the griddle ignites with bold flavor. From smoky peppers to bright heat and layered seasoning, this exploration celebrates the art of balancing fire and flavor. Home cooks experiment with spice to create dishes that bring warmth and excitement to every bite.

programme.title: Griddle Nation
programme.desc: Imagination meets the warmth of the griddle in dishes designed for kids’ tastes. Playful shapes, bright flavors, and familiar favorites come together to charm even the pickiest palates with every bite.

programme.title: Up the Dish
programme.desc: Host Tiera Singh goes on a South American tour with Peruvian Ceviche. Marinated citrusy seabass and shrimp swim in a sea of sweet potatoes and creamy avocado - it's hard to beat! But Poppy Sandler takes a shot, and lands on Grilled Shrimp with a smoky charred Corn and Avocado salad, topped with garlic and lemon grilled jumbo shrimp. Next, Missy Hui indulges her sweet tooth with Vegan Key Lime Tarts. Her trick? Using avocado for extra cream and extra green. Branzino with Shishito and Mango is how Micah Trainor scales it up - her whole-fish-dish is pan-roasted and spiced with chaat masala, topped with mangoes and blistered shishito peppers for a five-star feast!

programme.title: Up the Dish
programme.desc: How do you make any lasagna recipe better? Ask Tiera Singh to take it up, of course! Her 'Nonna-style' bechamel and Bolognese-based Lasagna is topped with melty mozzarella for a luscious and luxuriant dish. Tiera tosses it over to Poppy Sandler, whose sideways Seafood Lasagna mixes up mussels, shrimp, and a briny bechamel sauce - yum! Next, Micah Trainor makes a dressed-up vegetarian version of the classic - her Zucchini-Mushroom Lasagna Rolls with tangy sundried tomatoes, savoury ricotta cheese, and earthy mushroom duxelles is simply superlative. Missy Hui hits the sweet spot with her Chocolate Raspberry Dessert Lasagna; layers of rich white chocolate, thick raspberry coulis, and velvety mascarpone cheese come together for a dessert that's in the stratosphere!

programme.title: Up the Dish
programme.desc: In this episode of Up the Dish, host Missy Hui goes back to her childhood with Chicken and Dumplings - comfort food for every age! The chicken-dumpling journey doesn't end there; Micah Trainor turns the dish upside down and inside out with her Chinese-inspired Chicken-Chive Gyoza, a crunchy-crispy dim sum delight, with an ovERS-the-top chili sauce! How does Poppy Sandler spice things up? Herb Gnudi with Red Pepper Sauce, a spicy-savoury dumpling that's coated in nutty brown butter and creamy ricotta cheese for extra oomph. Tiera Singh finishes with another European dish - Spinach Spaetzle and Crispy Prosciutto, straight from Germany! Chewy spaetzle, fresh spinach, crispy ham - this textural treat doesn't let up!

programme.title: Up the Dish
programme.desc: Host Tiera Singh gets the show on the road with her Italian- inspired Fennel Orange Salad. Seared sushi-grade tuna rests on top of a crunchy, zesty, tangy fennel and orange salad with a honey-vinegar dressing to sweeten it up! Micah Trainor's revamp? Five Spice Duck a L'Orange with a sugary caramelized orange sauce and duck fat fried potatoes - is it French? Chinese? Who knows, but it's delicious! Next, Poppy Sandler pulls off a vegetarian variation, with a Roasted Veggie Bouillabaisse, complete with musky morels, umami-packed kombu, and tender asparagus. Missy Hui finishes up with her Orange Licorice Panna Cotta - aromatic orange blossom honey and licorice-like fennel pollen make the dish a decadent denouement to this episode of Up the Dish!

programme.title: The new era of design

programme.title: Catwalks
programme.desc: Sfilate integrali direttamente dalle passerelle di Milano, Parigi, New York, Londra

programme.title: Kapas Biru Waterfall: Indonesia
programme.desc: Escape to Indonesia with tropical vegetation and a rushing waterfall.

programme.title: Spirit Lake Relaxation
programme.desc: Relax for 3 hours at the gorgeous Spirit Lake, in Mount St Helen's Washington with this soothing dynamic nature film containing only the pure sounds of Nature!

programme.title: Underwater Wonders
programme.desc: Highlighting the abundant and varied flora and fauna that dwells underwater in several oceans around the world.

programme.title: Four Seasons: New Zealand
programme.desc: A look at New Zealand from four different angles: summer, fall, winter and spring.

programme.title: Rushing Rocky Stream: Hawaii
programme.desc: Along the Pipiwai Trail, take an unforgettable walk through Hawaii's immense and picture-perfect bamboo forest.

programme.title: Yellow Boat: Îles de la Madeleine
programme.desc: A yellow boat sits in swaying grass near the coast of Îles de la Madeleine, Quebec.

programme.title: Above the Tulip Fields
programme.desc: Tulip fields as seen from a drone perspective in the windmill-filled country of Holland.

programme.title: New Chums Beach: New Zealand
programme.desc: New Chums Beach offers a unique and idyllic coastal experience, combining natural beauty, tranquility, and seclusion; it's the perfect backdrop for nature lovers seeking the peaceful and serene with tranquil ocean views.

programme.title: Redwood Coast: California
programme.desc: Exploring forests with some of the biggest trees in the world.

programme.title: A Day in Paradise
programme.desc: Filmed in 2013 by David Huting, the tiny island is hidden off of the northeastern coast of the outer Fiji Islands (near Taveuni), and boasts a perfect white sandy beach, groves of palm trees, and a majestic lagoon that shelters the island from all sides. It has more turtle visitors than humans!

programme.title: Dorothy Falls & Hokitika Gorge: New Zealand
programme.desc: The blue-green waters of the Hokitika Gorge. The romance of Dorothy falls in the rain or on a bright sunny day.

programme.title: Te Urewera National Park: New Zealand
programme.desc: Taking in the many views of Te Urewera National Park's falls, from closeups of the rapid cascades to far-away vantages from the park's highest peaks.

programme.title: Islands of Paradise
programme.desc: The beautiful Yasawa islands of Fiji.

programme.title: Atuh Beach: Indonesia
programme.desc: Take a tour of beautiful Indonesian waters, surrounded by impressive rock formations.

programme.title: Auckland Beaches: New Zealand
programme.desc: The relaxing vibe of a day at the beach, with a tour of the breathtaking coastlines near Auckland.

programme.title: Ditaytayan Island: Philippines
programme.desc: Taking a look at Ditaytayan Island in the Philippines, from bird's-eye views of the coast to close-ups of the waves lapping on the shores.

programme.title: Fly Over Paradise: Philippines
programme.desc: A journey to discover tropical beaches.

programme.title: Flying Over Bermuda
programme.desc: Soar over Bermuda, a voyage that explores lush land and turquoise waters.

programme.title: Cadlao Lagoon: Philippines
programme.desc: Experiencing the beauty of a turquoise lagoon flanked by impressive, vegetation-covered, mountains.

programme.title: Rushing Rocky Stream: Hawaii
programme.desc: Along the Pipiwai Trail, take an unforgettable walk through Hawaii's immense and picture-perfect bamboo forest.

programme.title: Coral Kingdom
programme.desc: Arrangements of colorful coral sway to the rhythm of the tide, with aquatic visitors stopping by to enjoy the scenery.

programme.title: Wells Gray Falls
programme.desc: A peaceful soundtrack with a waterfall tumbling over the edge of a cliff.

programme.title: Waterfalls of the World 2
programme.desc: Places like Iguazu Falls in Argentina, Patagonia in Chile, Banff and Jasper in Alberta, McWay Falls and Hidden Falls in Big Sur, California, Columbia River Falls in Oregon, and more, paired with soothing ambient music by Relax Moods.

programme.title: A Day on the Napali Coast
programme.desc: An ambient nature film features the sights and sounds of the 11-mile Kalalau Trail on the Hawaiian island of Kauai.

programme.title: Lakeside Campfire: Quebec
programme.desc: Listening to the elegance and warmth of the crackling fire in Entrelacs, Quebec.

programme.title: Wonders of Nature
programme.desc: Immerse yourself in the magical and relaxing wonders of nature with an ambient relaxation video featuring world-class music by Darshan Ambient; in it, you'll be transported to over 30 different natural locations around the world.

programme.title: Dolphin Voyage Relaxation
programme.desc: Underwater cinematography, a synergistic blend of audio including underwater nature sounds, and calming ambient music combine to induce relaxation in the deep blue calming ocean with a pod of friendly dolphins.

programme.title: Glacial Beauty: Newfoundland
programme.desc: Admiring the quiet and glacial beauty of icebergs drifting across the bay at Newfoundland's Amherst Cove.

programme.title: Serene Waters: Diablo Lake, Washington
programme.desc: The serene calm of the Diablo Lake, surrounded by majestic mountains.

programme.title: A Day in San Diego
programme.desc: Featuring 12 different 15-minute scenes from the San Diego's beaches, Coronado Beach, Pacific Beach, Mission Beach, Blacks Beach, Ocean Beach, Sunset Cliffs, Torrey Pines and more.

programme.title: Earth From Above
programme.desc: Traveling the world with a birds-eye-view perspective of scenic wonders.

programme.title: Ninety Mile Beach: New Zealand
programme.desc: The almost never-ending paradise of one of New Zealand's most breathtaking beaches.

programme.title: Senaru Waterfalls: Indonesia
programme.desc: Discovering the beauty of waterfalls cascading from a sheer cliff, nestled in lush vegetation.

programme.title: A Day in the Caribbean
programme.desc: The tropical island of Antigua.

programme.title: Emerald Lake: French Alps
programme.desc: A remarkable view of the emerald water of Lake Castillon nestled in the French Alps.

programme.title: Tranquil Tide: Ca'te d'Azur
programme.desc: The medieval town of Menton on the French Côte d'Azur, where the sun glistens on iridescent water.

programme.title: Lombok Beaches: Indonesia
programme.desc: Experiencing the beaches of an Indonesian island set to a peaceful soundtrack.

programme.title: Beach Paradise: Hawaii
programme.desc: Spending a restful moment in Maui, a tropical paradise of gleaming white sand beaches and turquoise waters.

programme.title: Benang Kelambu & Stokel: Indonesia
programme.desc: Experience a waterfall flowing down a forest wall, surrounded by overgrown vines.

programme.title: Dramatic Coasts: Portugal
programme.desc: The raw nature of Sagres, Portugal, a destination of dramatic landscapes and pristine beaches.

programme.title: Flying Over Big Sur
programme.desc: Soar over California's Big Sur for an hour of relaxation with Big Sur From Above, Nature Relaxation's 2019 drone film; see iconic sites like McWay Falls and Bixby Bridge from an aerial view.

programme.title: Flying Over Greece
programme.desc: Journey through the landscapes of Greece from a bird's-eye-view.

programme.title: Chemin Pealey: Îles de la Madeleine
programme.desc: Admiring the tranquility of water.

programme.title: Serene Waters: Diablo Lake, Washington
programme.desc: The serene calm of the Diablo Lake, surrounded by majestic mountains.

programme.title: Forest in the Sea
programme.desc: Explore the kelp forest off the California coast, that supports more than 100,000 creatures.

programme.title: Beach Paradise: Hawaii
programme.desc: Spending a restful moment in Maui, a tropical paradise of gleaming white sand beaches and turquoise waters.

programme.title: Golden Waves Crashing at Sunset
programme.desc: The shores of the iconic Baker Beach in San Francisco with a sunset scene filmed in high definition. Views of the Marin headlands and the Golden Gate Bridge.

programme.title: Algarve: Portugal
programme.desc: Relax along the coastal cliffs in Portugal with this stunning 4K ambient nature film showcasing the stunning sandstone cliffs, glistening blue waters, peaceful waves and birds flying.

programme.title: New Zealand Gardens
programme.desc: The filmmaker explores the breathtaking gardens of New Zealand and provides the diversity and beauty of these enchanting havens.

programme.title: Opera - Salome
programme.desc: The 87th Festival of the Maggio Musicale Fiorentino opens with Richard Strauss's Salome, conducted by Alexander Soddy. Directed by Emma Dante, this new production features Lidia Fridman as Salome, Anna Maria Chiuri as Herodias, and Nikolai Schukoff as Herodes. The performance blends Strauss's intense musical drama with a fresh and dynamic staging.

programme.title: Mediterranean Yoga Release Stress from the Body

programme.title: Med Minds 06 Jean de Valette
programme.desc: Jean Parisot de Valette, the steadfast Grand Master of Malta, led the Knights with courage, defended his island, and saved Europe as we know it.

programme.title: Med Nations 01 Italy
programme.desc: The heart of Italy! A place where old empires, the brilliance of the Renaissance, and modern passion all come together. Italy shows itself through its traditions, flavours, and lively way of life. From the timeless beauty of Rome to the artistic heart of Florence and the vibrant energy of Naples, Italy is alive with life. It's a journey through a country that has had a profound impact on art, cuisine, and culture throughout the Mediterranean and beyond.

programme.title: Med Destinations Rimini

programme.title: Mediterranean Kitchen Malta
programme.desc: Chef Marvin joins Chef Kurt in the kitchen to cook up a real Maltese feast. Together they rustle up traditional snails in a rich tomato sauce, fresh bigilla dip and classic ftira bread, finishing with sweet imqaret for dessert. It is a beautiful reminder of how food brings people together, full of laughter, conversation, and timeless recipes.

programme.title: Med Destinations Ancona

programme.title: Gold Cuisine
programme.desc: Step into a world of hearty and inventive flavors with oven-baked meatballs paired with succulent pork tenderloin, followed by a creamy and naturally sweet avocado and date mousse for dessert. A delightful combination of savory comfort and unique, indulgent sweetness.

programme.title: Split (Firule)

programme.title: Malini Angelica Lebanon
programme.desc: Malini travels through Lebanon, exploring some of the country’s most beautiful sites, including Baalbek, Jeita Grotto, Harissa, Byblos, Batroun, and Tripoli.

programme.title: Med Whats On Extraordinary Events
programme.desc: Spring is transforming the Mediterranean into a massive stage for high-tech art and spectacular outdoor events. We begin at the Ta Qali National Park for the Bloom Festival, where wellness and music create a vibrant sanctuary in the heart of Malta. From there, we head into the tunnels and forts of Valletta and Birgu for the Malta Biennale 2026 to discover contemporary installations hidden inside historic sites rarely seen by the public. Our journey takes us to the cliffs of Gozo as giant creative designs fill the sky for the International Kite and Wind Festival before we land in Venice to witness the world class creativity of the 61st International Art Exhibition. This is a look at a region in the middle of a stunning cultural rebirth.

programme.title: Med Destinations Ios
programme.desc: Discover the lively spirit and scenic beauty of Ios on Med Destinations. From crystal-clear beaches to winding streets and sunset views, Ios combines vibrant island life with the tranquility of the Aegean Sea.

programme.title: Rick Steves Europe
programme.desc: In Milan we'll take a peek at Italy's highest fashion, fanciest delis, grandest cemetery, and greatest opera house...not to mention Leonardo's "Last Supper." Then we'll cruise Lake Como, settling down in the lakeside village of Varenna.

programme.title: Med Nations 02 Greece
programme.desc: Greece has a culture that has been shaped by hundreds of years of ideas, traditions, and life on the sea. This trip shows how Greece still inspires the Mediterranean and the world through its food, music, and long history.

programme.title: Rick Steves Europe
programme.desc: Naples is Italy in the extreme. We’ll prowl backstreet fish markets and dodge speedy Vespas before indulging in pizza where it was invented. Afterward, we'll climb to the top of nearby Mount Vesuvius, then wander through the ruins of Pompeii, the Roman town it destroyed.

programme.title: Med Destinations Delos
programme.desc: Step into the myth and mystery of Delos with Med Destinations. This tiny island, rich in archaeological wonders and legendary tales, offers a unique glimpse into Greece’s ancient past amid stunning coastal vistas.

programme.title: Malini Angelica Calabria
programme.desc: Malini explores Calabria, a hidden gem in Southern Italy. While its coastal towns like Tropea and Scilla boast turquoise seas, Calabria’s true charm lies in its rural interior, where traditions like handmade pasta, cheese, and salami are still alive. She visits ghost villages like Pentedattilo in the Aspromonte mountains and experiences authentic Calabrian culture, food, and hospitality, hosted by Calabria Etnica.

programme.title: Sapiens I Collassi Delle Civilta Del Passato Part 1
programme.desc: Sapiens I Collassi Delle Civilta Del Passato Part 1

programme.title: Dates that made history
programme.desc: Mansa Musa's Pilgrimage to Mecca explores the extraordinary journey of Mansa Musa, the emperor of Mali, to Mecca in 1324. Highlighting the wealth and power of the Mali Empire as Musa’s caravan traverses the Sahara, spreading wealth and religious influence. It examines the impact of his pilgrimage on both the Islamic world and Africa’s place in global history. Through this journey, Mansa Musa’s legacy of generosity and cultural exchange becomes legendary.

programme.title: The Great Greek Myths
programme.desc: The tale of Tartarus: The Damned of the Earth descends into the deepest abyss of the Greek underworld, where the most cursed souls are imprisoned. Here dwell legendary figures punished for defying the gods, bound by eternal torment. This dark realm serves as a chilling reminder of divine justice and the price of hubris. A myth that explores punishment, morality, and the shadow side of eternity.

programme.title: Mediterranean Yoga Feel safe in your body
programme.desc: Mediterranean Yoga is a series that blends Embodiment Yoga with the peaceful beauty of the Mediterranean. programme.title: Concerto - OSN Fabio Luisi e Alexander Gadjiev Torino 2022
programme.desc: On April 28, 2022, esteemed conductor Fabio Luisi and acclaimed pianist Alexander Gadjiev took the stage at the Auditorium Rai "Arturo Toscanini" in Turin, accompanied by the Orchestra Sinfonica Nazionale della Rai. Gadjiev delivered a masterful performance of Chopin’s Piano Concerto No. 2, while Luisi led a captivating interpretation of Strauss’ "Aus Italien".

programme.title: Whats On Trade, Exhibitions and Unique Experiences
programme.desc: There is a unique energy that takes over the Mediterranean as the summer heat begins to build. This week, we are bypassing the usual tourist routes to find the heartbeat of the region through its most spectacular festivals. Imagine the sun setting over a lake in Tuscany as the Puccini Festival opens its 72nd season with the powerful melodies of Turandot. From that timeless elegance, we head straight into the high energy of Malta, where the Isle of MTV and the 10th anniversary of the Glitch Festival are turning the islands into a global hub for electronic music. We also explore the stunning heights of the Spanish Pyrenees for the floating stage at Pirineos Sur and hit the iconic cliffs of Santorini for the urban vibes of the Supa Dupa Fly Festival. This is a journey through a region that knows exactly how to celebrate its past while dancing firmly into the future.

programme.title: Med Minds 01 Caravaggio
programme.desc: Caravaggio, the rebellious Mediterranean painter, lived fiercely and left a legacy as bold as his character.

programme.title: Med Nations 03 Turkey
programme.desc: Turkey, where cultures, continents, and centuries of history all come together. Turkey has a lot of different traditions, flavours, and history that have been shaped by powerful empires and cultural crossroads that have been around for hundreds of years. You can see this from the busy streets of Istanbul to the sunny Mediterranean coast.

programme.title: Mediterranean Kitchen Italy
programme.desc: Chef Kurt welcomes Chef Fausto into the kitchen to learn the craft of a Roman icon: authentic Pasta all'Amatriciana, perfected with crispy guanciale and rich pecorino cheese. programme.title: Jesenice
programme.desc: In the hillside village of Staro Selo Jesenice, with views of the central Dalmatian islands, we found stone houses, a bocce court, and a traditional tavern run by Ante Bajso. There, he treated us to a Dalmatian classic—meat slow-cooked under a bell-shaped lid, peka.

programme.desc: Malini Angelica explores Menorca, from Mahon’s markets and history to Ciutadella’s gothic charm and northern beaches. In Es Migjorn Gran, she hikes to ancient caves, visits Binigaus beach, and also learns to make Mahon style cheese.

programme.title: Whats On Lights, Cinema, Mediterranean
programme.desc: The Mediterranean screens its stories this week. Malta hosts the Mediterranean Film Festival, while Italy Venice Film Festival and Med Film Festival bring glamour and regional storytelling to iconic locations. France Cannes Film Festival shines globally, Spain International Fantastic Film Festival of Catalonia celebrates genre cinema, and Morocco Amazigh Film Festival showcases North African voices. Turkey Adana Golden Boll Film Festival and Croatia Zagreb Film Festival round out a cinematic journey across the Med.

programme.desc: Exploring Croatia, Montenegro, and Bosnia, we’ll get to know the three major groups of the former Yugoslavia, Croats, Serbs, and Bosniaks, before enjoying a bit of fun in the sun along the Adriatic in Dubrovnik.

programme.title: Malini Angelica Marseille
programme.desc: Malini Angelica travels to Marseille, France’s vibrant Mediterranean port city. She explores its historic Old Port, colorful neighborhoods, and bustling markets, discovering the city’s unique blend of French and North African influences. From sampling local dishes like bouillabaisse to soaking in seaside views and street art, Malini uncovers what makes Marseille one of France’s most dynamic and diverse cities.

programme.title: Origini
programme.desc: Francesco Gasparri and Valentina Caruso explore Taranto, founded by Spartan settlers in 706 BC and soon a bustling Mediterranean hub. Gasparri traces the city’s history through the Aragonese castle, revealing remnants of ancient Taranto. Caruso visits the Archaeological Park of Egnatia, where the ancient Via Traiana, built by Emperor Trajan, still stands—a route once used by pilgrims to the Holy Land. Salento's traditions, including the Notte della Taranta dance, preserve the legacy of this rich past, as explained by prima ballerina Lucia Scarabina.

programme.title: Marvels of the Mediterranean
programme.desc: The Museum of Fine Arts of Algiers is a veritable treasure. Since 1930, it has enjoyed special status as a meeting place of Eastern and Western art

programme.title: Dates that made history
programme.desc: Mansa Musa's Pilgrimage to Mecca explores the extraordinary journey of Mansa Musa, the emperor of Mali, to Mecca in 1324. Highlighting the wealth and power of the Mali Empire as Musa’s caravan traverses the Sahara, spreading wealth and religious influence. It examines the impact of his pilgrimage on both the Islamic world and Africa’s place in global history. Through this journey, Mansa Musa’s legacy of generosity and cultural exchange becomes legendary.

programme.title: The Great Greek Myths
programme.desc: The story of Theseus: The Ravages of Oversight follows the heroic journey of the Athenian prince as he faces a deadly challenge in the Labyrinth of Crete. With courage and cunning, he confronts a fearsome threat at the heart of the maze. But even heroes are not immune to the consequences of a single forgotten detail. A tale of bravery, fate, and the unseen weight of human error.

programme.title: Concerto – OSN Gatti Bruckner Wagner Torino
programme.desc: In January 2022, Daniele Gatti conducted the Orchestra Sinfonica Nazionale della Rai at the Turin Auditorium, performing Wagner’s "Parsifal" excerpts and Bruckner’s Symphony No. 9. The programme highlighted Wagner's meditative Prelude to Act III and Good Friday Spell, followed by Bruckner’s profound Ninth Symphony, marked by its emotional depth and unfinished final movement.

programme.title: Whats On Trade, Exhibitions and Unique Experiences
programme.desc: There is a unique energy that takes over the Mediterranean as the summer heat begins to build. This week, we are bypassing the usual tourist routes to find the heartbeat of the region through its most spectacular festivals. Imagine the sun setting over a lake in Tuscany as the Puccini Festival opens its 72nd season with the powerful melodies of Turandot. From that timeless elegance, we head straight into the high energy of Malta, where the Isle of MTV and the 10th anniversary of the Glitch Festival are turning the islands into a global hub for electronic music. We also explore the stunning heights of the Spanish Pyrenees for the floating stage at Pirineos Sur and hit the iconic cliffs of Santorini for the urban vibes of the Supa Dupa Fly Festival. This is a journey through a region that knows exactly how to celebrate its past while dancing firmly into the future.

programme.title: Outdoor and Nature
programme.desc: OUTDOOR & Nature, a weekly program that highlights sports, outdoor activities, and the breathtaking scenery of Cyprus, will introduce you to the great outdoors. Each episode highlights government initiatives, community organisations, and volunteer efforts to preserve Cyprus's natural heritage while examining nature, wildlife, and conservation. Take a trip of inspiration, exploration, and adventure with hosts Panicos Pilalis and Aristo Aristeidou. Listen and get motivated to go on your own outdoor adventure in Cyprus!

programme.title: MED Destination VENICE
programme.desc: WITH VO

programme.title: Jesenice
programme.desc: In the hillside village of Staro Selo Jesenice, with views of the central Dalmatian islands, we found stone houses, a bocce court, and a traditional tavern run by Ante Bajso. There, he treated us to a Dalmatian classic—meat slow-cooked under a bell-shaped lid, peka.

programme.title: Med Destination - Dubrovnik
programme.desc: WITH VO

programme.title: Malini Angelica Lisbon
programme.desc: Malini Angelica explores Lisbon, the vibrant capital of Portugal. Once founded by the Phoenicians and shaped by Romans, Moors, and explorers of the Golden Age, Lisbon’s layered history is reflected in its culture and charm. During her visit, the city comes alive with the Festas Populares de Lisboa — a June celebration of Saint Anthony filled with music, dancing, and barbecued sardines. Malini samples local favorites like grilled sardines, bifanas, and the famous pastel de nata, and experiences the soulful beauty of Fado music. Lisbon’s warmth, flavor, and festive spirit show why it remains one of Europe’s most beloved cities.

programme.title: Rick Steves Europe
programme.desc: In Castile - Roman Segovia, walled Ávila, youthful Salamanca, and holy Toledo - we marvel at art, pop perky pickles, ramble ramparts, and enjoy Europe's most enchanting square.

programme.title: Concerto – OSN Gatti Bruckner Wagner Torino
programme.desc: In January 2022, Daniele Gatti conducted the Orchestra Sinfonica Nazionale della Rai at the Turin Auditorium, performing Wagner’s "Parsifal" excerpts and Bruckner’s Symphony No. 9. The programme highlighted Wagner's meditative Prelude to Act III and Good Friday Spell, followed by Bruckner’s profound Ninth Symphony, marked by its emotional depth and unfinished final movement.

programme.title: Whats On Trade, Exhibitions and Unique Experiences
programme.desc: There is a unique energy that takes over the Mediterranean as the summer heat begins to build. This week, we are bypassing the usual tourist routes to find the heartbeat of the region through its most spectacular festivals. We also explore the stunning heights of the Spanish Pyrenees for the floating stage at Pirineos Sur and hit the iconic cliffs of Santorini for the urban vibes of the Supa Dupa Fly Festival. This is a journey through a region that knows exactly how to celebrate its past while dancing firmly into the future.

programme.title: Backpack
programme.desc: Alexandra, in this episode visits Dubrovnik, where she explores a war museum, the dramatic monuments, mesmerising landscapes and tries water sports.

programme.title: Viajar Peru
programme.desc: Milagros’s first stop to showcase her country is at Ica on Peru’s southern coast known for its vineyards and for the desert oasis, Huacachina.

programme.title: 10 Days Estonia
programme.desc: Despite the boom of the 21st century development, Tallinn, the 1000-year-old capital of Estonia, safeguards its UNESCO-listed medieval Old Town, one of Europe’s most complete walled cities. Marco Biagioli and Erica Melargo, a famous Italian nomad couple, lose themselves in its fairy-tale charms, and find out its legends. They also visit iconic attractions such as Alexander Nevsky Cathedral and Raeapteek, the oldest pharmacy in Europe that has continuously been in business in the same building.

programme.title: Xplore Belgium
programme.desc: The food, the beers, the chocolate and the fries. Bruges is one of the cities that seemed to have skipped 21st century altogether.

programme.title: City Breaks
programme.desc: A quick escape to Jaipur introduces Asmita to the stark contrast in the old and new sides of Rajasthan’s capital city. On the one hand, she has a tryst with the global cosmopolis that attracts business and luxury travelers from all over the world. On the other hand, the regal monuments of the region reveal to her the stories of the kings who built them.

programme.title: Quest
programme.desc: From a rich lamb curry to a hearty pulao made with rare mushrooms, Alex learns to make the secret recipes of a local family in Delhi.

programme.title: Mr. & Mrs. Roy on the Maharaja's Express
programme.desc: Rohit and Manasi are on the Maharajas’ Express. Their first destination is Udaipur, where they bump into an old friend who knows all their secrets.

programme.title: Viajar Peru
programme.desc: In Arequipa, Milagros has set out to peel back the layers of her country’s history.

programme.title: 10 Days Estonia
programme.desc: Tallinn, Estonia’s capital, is known for its quaint, medieval Old Town but Italian nomad couple, Marco and Erica discover other sides to the city by visiting offbeat attractions such as the Seaplane Harbour Museum and Telliskivi Creative City, a hub of artists. They also tuck into a medieval feast at Olde Hansa Inn. Following this, they have an off-roading adventure at Aidu oil shale quarry.

programme.title: Xplore Belgium
programme.desc: Market square of Bruges is lined up with restaurants and cafes. The city is also home to golden fries or french fries, but are originated in Belgium.

programme.title: City Breaks
programme.desc: From stunning palaces to ornate stepwells, Asmita finds herself soaking in the romance of Bundi, a charming 14th century town in Rajasthan.

programme.title: Quest
programme.desc: From papad ka paratha to lehsun ki kheer, Chef Aditya Jaimini shows Alex Outhwaite how to make some of the unusual dishes from Indian cuisine.

programme.title: Backpack
programme.desc: Explore the beautiful Dalmatian coast with a little history of Split with Alex and stay tuned as next she explores the port city of Salona.

programme.title: Viajar Peru
programme.desc: Amidst the sapphire expanse of Lake Titicaca, Milagros embarks on a journey of reconnecting with her roots. Delving into the secrets of the Uros, she unveils their timeless artistry in building floating islands, a testament to human resilience. But as she travels to Amantani Island, an enigma awaits—a forgotten temple, rituals that date back to the pre-Incan times, chapters of Peru’s lesser-explored history waiting to unfold.

programme.title: 10 Days Estonia
programme.desc: Marco and Erica get to know about the dark history of Estonia while exploring the remains of Narva, the country’s third largest city at its easternmost limit. From the fortress of Narva and the bastions of Narva to an abandoned textile mill, everything tells them about the intriguing story of the town. They also learn about the Russian and ‘alien’ people living here.

programme.title: Xplore Belgium
programme.desc: Renowned as the world's leading city of diamonds, it also holds about possessing the second largest port in whole of Europe.

programme.title: City Breaks
programme.desc: Bikaner is known to most people as the city of world-famous savoury snacks. When Asmita goes there for a whirlwind trip, however, she discovers that there is a lot more to Rajasthan’s Red City than meets the eye. From the miniature paintings that can be found on the walls of palaces and mansions across the city to the elaborate architecture of the former havelis of merchants, she finds out that Bikaner is an art lover’s paradise.

programme.title: Quest
programme.desc: Amritsar,where recipes have been passed down generations. Alex explores it in Quest.

programme.title: Backpack
programme.desc: Watch a musical instrument powered by sea waves and learn how glassware was made in Roman era. This and more with Alexandra in Zadar episode

programme.title: Viajar Peru
programme.desc: Milagros visits the ancient city of Cusco just in time for the legendary Inti Raymi Festival, a gateway to the Inca empire's beliefs. Hidden in the vibrant festivities, which continue into the night, are glimpses into the lives led by the people during the Inca era.

programme.title: 10 Days Estonia
programme.desc: Marco and Erica soak in the vitality of the student town, Tartu, and experience its eccentricities at the Leaning House and the AHHAA Science Centre. Following this, they visit Obinista, the cultural centre of Setomaa, where the Seto community introduces them to the traditional ways of dressing, singing and dancing. They also indulge in a traditional smoke sauna at nearby Võromaa.

programme.title: Xplore Belgium
programme.desc: Antwerp has grown on the banks of the Scheldt River that alongwith the Meuse and the Rhine forms the biggest estuary in western Europe.

programme.title: World Spas
programme.desc: Jamie Srubis tries a massage inspired by the waves of the sea and a chocolate vanilla body wrap at the Gaya Island Resort in Malaysia.

programme.title: Quest
programme.desc: You won't find anything more comforting than food made from scratch.Join Alex in Hoshiarpur as she finds old cuisines.

programme.title: Backpack
programme.desc: Alexandra in this episode explores Plitvice, where she visits a national park, interacts with locals, tastes local wine and then leaves for Zagreb.

programme.title: Viajar Peru
programme.desc: Scaling rocks and defying heights, Milagros finds herself suspended 1200 feet above the ground at the Sacred Valley of the Incas.

programme.title: 10 Days Estonia
programme.desc: Embracing Estonia’s largest area of swamps, meadows and waterside forests, Soomaa National Park is the second largest national park in the country. While trying out bog walking and camping over here, Marco and Erica experience what is known as the “fifth season” in Estonia, the season of floods. They also explore Tallinn’s history with espionage, and go on to soak in the romance at its Christmas Market.

programme.title: Xplore Belgium
programme.desc: No matter where you look, you will see impressive, interesting architecture that will catch your eye. Until 1550 Ghent was one of the largest cities.

programme.title: World Spas
programme.desc: Travel to Da Nang this week with World Spas and take a peek into the life of the pampered few.

programme.title: Quest
programme.desc: Alex Outhwaite meets a Nawab in Lucknow, who shows her how to make Gosht Mawazad, a decadent Awadhi dish with mutton and dry fruits.

programme.title: Xplore Belgium
programme.desc: Brussels is one of the most cosmopolitan cities of Belgium and also its economic and political center. There are way too many things to do here.

programme.desc: Rohit and Manasi are on the Maharajas’ Express. Their first destination is Udaipur, where they bump into an old friend who knows all their secrets.

programme.desc: Embarking on a homecoming adventure in the heart of the Peruvian Amazonas, Milagros explores her roots like never before.

programme.title: World Heritage

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Robbie’s adventures in Perth kick off with him immersing himself in the historic grandeur of the Scone Castle, where a mysterious stone tells tales of Scottish monarchs. But the real thunder of the Highlands awaits at the Braemar Games gathering, where tartan meets brawn, and Robbie learns that embracing his Scottish side involves more than just kilts and castles.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Heather explores Novi Sad, the financial and industrial hub of Serbia.Starting with canoeing on Danube and later exploring the city and its landmarks.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Emeline Nsingi Nkosi goes to Santa Village at Rovaniemi, where she reads some of the letters that children from around the world write to him.

programme.title: Xplore Bulgaria
programme.desc: Luxurious hotels, thrilling water parks, inexpensive food and almost 300 days of bright sunny weather make Bulgaria’s biggest seaside resort, Sunny Beach popular for budget beach getaways. Catriona, however, stumbles onto the historic town of Nessebar, which is over 3000 years old, and realizes that there is more to the place than meets the eye. She goes on to visit the Castra Rubra winery, where she finds out about the history of Viticulture in Bulgaria and the process of wine making.

programme.title: City Breaks
programme.desc: Benafsha embarks on a whirlwind adventure in Ahmedabad. With just 48 hours in the city, she is determined to make the most of it, but how?

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: From exploring a vintage car museum to scaling a 5 meter ice-wall and sampling a rich bread cookie, Watch Alex exploring The Hague, also known as the “International city of peace”.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: A modern European oil city built on the back of a rich maritime heritage, Aberdeen is the energetic kingpin of northeast Scotland. While the boom times may come and go, the dynamic coastal metropolis never ceases to thrill with its free museums, outdoor adventures and natural attractions right on the city’s doorstep.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Heather explores Požarevac and visits the ancient town of Viminacium. Later visits National Museum, concluding with Majdanpek’s Rajkova Pecina Caves.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Emeline Nsingi Nkosi goes to Rovaniemi, where she visits a husky farm and finds out about about breeding and raising huskies.

programme.title: Xplore Bulgaria
programme.desc: For the people of Sofia, preserving its heritage is sacrosanct, and this can be seen in the way the city's infrastructure is built around its archeological treasures. Catriona realizes this when she roams around the cobblestone streets of the city. With every monument she visits, she finds herself transported to a different era, right from Perperikon fort, which narrate stories of the Byzantine empire, to Boyana church, which is home to some of the earliest examples of medieval artwork.

programme.title: Great World Hotels
programme.desc: From lazing in hammocks by the beach to sipping drinks on a private yacht, Kim realizes that Maldives is the perfect destination to unwind in style.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: From trying dishes like Stroopwafel and Palat Stoofvlees to visiting the Erasmus bridge, cube houses and wall painting on the streets, Alex explores Rotterdam.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Since medieval times Aberdeenshire and its northwestern neighbour Moray have been the richest and most fertile regions of the Highlands. Aberdeenshire is famed for its Aberdeen Angus beef cattle, its many fine castles and the prosperous 'granite city' of Aberdeen.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Explore Majdanpek with our host as the city brings the viewers closer to the treasures strewn across the Serbian countryside.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Levi is Finland's largest and fastest-growing ski destination. Emeline is here, to find out the exciting activities that Levi offers, in summer.

programme.title: Xplore Bhutan
programme.desc: With snow-capped peaks rising over primeval forests and traditional villages, Paro is like an Oriental Switzerland. On a visit to Paro, Radhika explores famous monasteries such as the Rinpung Dzong and Takstang Monastery, and learns the history and legends associated with them. Along the way, she finds out the secrets to the quintessential Bhutanese peace and contentment.

programme.title: Great World Hotels
programme.desc: An invigorating spa treatment, a cooking lesson, a snorkeling session – Kim has experiences in the Maldives that leave her completely rejuvenated.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: From trying Orange zest beer and traditional Dutch dishes to exploring St.Martin's Cathedral, Dom Tower and enjoying go karting and stand up paddle boat ride, watch Alex enjoy his trip in Utrecht.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: In the enchanting landscapes of Inverness, Robbie’s journey takes a whimsical turn: from eccentric to downright magical.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Heather explores Nis,one of the oldest cities in Europe.She visits a concentration camp and later paraglides to end by watching a musical performance.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Emeline goes to Ruka-Kuusamo, where she tries river rafting. She also meets a reindeer herder,who tells her about the unique ways of herding reindeer.

programme.title: Xplore Bhutan
programme.desc: Actress and travel enthusiast, Radhika Bangia explores the enigma that is Bhutan, when she visits Thimpu, the capital city with no traffic lights and no skyscrapers invading the skyline. Here, she checks out significant sights such as the Tibetan-style National Memorial Chorten, the National Library and the Folk Heritage Museum in order to understand more about the beliefs, history, architecture and cuisine of Bhutan.

programme.title: World Heritage
programme.desc: Explore the ancient heritage sites of Abu Simbel, the Philae complex of Nubia and the Hatshepsut Temple to unravel the mystical stories behind the ancient temples of Egypt.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: Alex tries traditional Dutch food in Leiden, learns about Dutch history at Prinsenhof Museum, visits a Gothic church, a local cheese market and Delftware factory at Gouda.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: The grand finale of Robbie’s wild homecoming unfolds amidst the rugged beauty of the Isle of Skye.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Continue exploring Serbia with Heather as she visits the Manasija monastery, Resavska caves, ending with a joyride onboard Eight of Sargan rail line.

programme.title: With Love From Mauritius
programme.desc: Krisann and Nathan’s vacation in Mauritius begins in Le Morne, where an unexpected adventure awaits the latter.

programme.title: Xplore Bhutan
programme.desc: Known as Druk Yul or the “Land of the Thunder Dragon”, Bhutan is the country of mountains, monasteries and mystery. Radhika Bangia finds this to be true when she visits Punakha, the former capital of Bhutan, where she marvels at the architecture of Punakha Dzong and gets to know the story behind the two rivers, Mo Chhu and Po Chhu. She also explores the sacred temples and Mebar Tsho lake of Bumthang, the religious heart of Bhutan, and gets to know the local legends.

programme.title: World Spas
programme.desc: Jamie Srubis tries out spa treatments, which are inspired by the Dead Sea and the desert surroundings at Hotel Kempinski Ishtar in Jordan.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: After Burj Khalifa, Alex has a traditional Emirati platter, visits the gold market and skiing rink, and goes for a camel safari and camping.

programme.title: City break
programme.desc: Nestled in the mighty Alps, tour the Austrian city of Innsbruck with Sabrina and explore a town that’s alpine-urban yet traditional in nature.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: In her final leg of journey, Heather visits the Uvac canyon, tries locally prepared pie, ending with Kopaonik National Park and Jelovarnik waterfalls.

programme.title: With Love From Mauritius
programme.desc: On an island-hopping adventure in Mauritius, Krisann and Nathan find out that time works differently on these islands.

programme.title: XP Guide
programme.desc: A homely place where the aroma of freshly baked bread greets you as people smoothly glide along on their bicycles. Welcome to Copenhagen.

programme.title: Unwind
programme.desc: Indeya explores the Cinnamon plantations, tries a local dish and witnesses a traditional dance performance.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: Alex has varied experiences in Dubai like trying Lebanese cheese bread, visiting a coffee museum, going to IMG Worlds of Adventure and more.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: In Amsterdam, Alex tries Bitterballen, cannabis cake and visits Heineken Experience brewery. He explores Zaanse Schans village with windmills and Coster Diamonds, the oldest diamond polishing factory.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Robbie’s journey to reconnect with his Scottish roots begins in Edinburgh, the stunning capital city perched on extinct volcanoes. Navigating the twists of the Royal Mile, strutting around the majestic Edinburgh castle and learning to sip scotch like a true Scotsman, he feels a tad closer to his heritage. The real fun kicks in, however, when he stumbles onto a rather flamboyant way to unleash his inner Highlander.

programme.title: Xplore Bulgaria
programme.desc: Like Rome, Plovdiv straddles seven hills; but as Europe’s oldest continuously inhabited city, it’s far more ancient. As Catriona takes a tour of the city to see its iconic attractions such as a Roman Amphitheatre, which was once the setting for gladiator fights, and the striking Baroque Saints Constantine and Helena Church, she begins to see how seamlessly modern life blends with the age-old ruins. Along the way, she realizes why Plovdiv is called the ‘Eternal City’ of Bulgaria.

programme.title: XP Guide
programme.desc: Stockholm, the capital and largest city of Sweden, was built on 14 Islands. This place is also sometimes referred as Venice of the north.

programme.desc: This exhilarating city of castles, beer and segway rides can take you on a break that will truly leave you catching your breath.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita Gente, dove incontra Jay Mitchel un americano appassionato di Italia al punto di aprire un suo ristorante in america per far apprezzare il cibo Italiano ad i suoi clienti.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage.Ornella Fado, visita il ristorante stellato Cascinale Novo, dove incontra chef Walter Ferreto e preparano delle tipiche ricette Piemontesi. Ornella inoltre visitare la casa del Trifolao di Natale Romagnolo per una caccia al tartufo Bianco.

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado si trova nel laboratorio di Urbani Truffle a New York ed invita i suoi amici chef per preparare insieme delle ricette da gustare durante le festività natalizie. In questa puntata Chef Pino Coladonato, Chef Marcello Russodivito e la giovane pizzaiola Giorgia Caporuscio. In questa edizione di Brindiamo! in my Kitchen ospite speciale la soprano Cristina Fontanelli e John La Barbera, oltre a vari ospiti del mondo dello spettacolo, e della moda. (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado si trova nel laboratorio di Urbani Truffle a New York per la serconda parte di Brindiamo! in my kitchen. I suoi amici chef in questa puntata son lo chef Raffaele Ronca, lo Chef Marco Chirico e il giovane pizzaiolo Ettore Toches. In questa edizione di Brindiamo! in my Kitchen la musica è dal vivo con la soprano Cristina Fontanelli ed il compositore Jhon La Barbera, tra gli ospiti di questa puntata il Fashion designer Domenico Vacca. (2016)

Visitando a midtown Chef Pino alla "La Masseria", chef Stefano e Franco a " Viceversa", a "PizzArte" incontra il pizzaiolo Roberto ed il proprietario Dario, a Queens visita "Ornella Trattoria" dove incontra Vincenzo e "Trattoria l'Incontro"dove incontra lo chef e proprietario Rocco. (2013/2014)

programme.title: Eccellenze Italiane con Piero Muscari intervista a Heinz Beck
programme.desc: Piero Muscari intervista Cesare Berlingeri, noto artista contemporaneo italiano. Cesare illustra l'importanza della sua arte e cosa vuole esprimere con la tecnica dei quadri piegati.

programme.title: Eccellenze Italiane con Piero Muscari intervista a Heinz Beck
programme.desc: Piero Muscari intervista Alberto Biasi, famoso artista italiano. Alberto racconta delle esperienze della sua infanzia e come queste hanno influenzato le sue opere e la sua arte.

programme.title: My Talented Friends Season 1
programme.desc: Ornella Fado introduce il suo amico Geniale Julian Gargiulo, un talentuosissimo compositore e pianista on uno spiccato senso dell'umore, Julian e` il protagnosita e regista di un documentario girato al Carnegie Hall di New York, durante una competizione ideada dal maestro Gargiiulo.

programme.title: My Talented Friends Season 1
programme.desc: Ornella Fado introuce il suo amico Geniale Marco Napoli, attore e regista televisivo e cinematografico. Il documentario di oggi vede come protagonista una giovane artista catanese Roberta Finocchiaro "Save lives with the Rhytm.

programme.title: My Talented Friends Season 1
programme.desc: Ornella Fado introuce il suo amico Geniale Marco Napoli, attore e regista televisivo e cinematografico. Il documentario di oggi vede come protagonista una giovane artista catanese Roberta Finocchiaro "Save lives with the Rhytm.

programme.title: Il CIRCOLO DEL BUONGUSTAI Con Fabio Campoli
programme.desc: Benvenuti a questo nuovo episodio del Circolo dei Buongustai! Cosa bolle in pentola? Oggi il nostro chef Fabio Campoli ci delizierà con tre ricette speciali: portafoglio di squacquerone, ciliegie e mortadella, riso rosso integrale con pollo e peperoni, diplomatico alle rose. Buona visione a tutti!

programme.title: Il CIRCOLO DEL BUONGUSTAI Con Fabio Campoli
programme.desc: Benvenuti a questo nuovo episodio del Circolo dei Buongustai! Cosa bolle in pentola? Oggi il nostro chef Fabio Campoli ci delizierà con tre ricette speciali: rotolo di riso, prosciutto e melone, risotto alla rucola con kiwi e vongole, e budino di riso. Buona visione a tutti!

programme.title: Change your Destiny
programme.desc: Nella prima puntata Antonio Fini ed i suoi giudici incontrano il primo gruppo di ballerini.Per essere selezionati per il prestigioso festival di danza in NEW YORK devono affrontare 3 sfide: presentare il loro pezzo migliore, la contemporary battle e la lezione di danza "tecnica Graham" con il maestro Antonio Fini.

programme.title: Change your Destiny
programme.desc: Continuano le audizioni di Antonio Fini e dei suoi giudici per selezionare i migliori danzatori da invitare al prestigioso festival di danza in NEW YORK. I ragazzi affrontano le tre sfide previste: portare il loro pezzo migliore, la contemporary battle e la lezione di Hip hop con Mirco Giordano e Marcello Gargarelli.

programme.title: Change your Destiny
programme.desc: Terza ed ultima puntata della serie. Antonio Fini ed i suoi giudici sono pronti: chi sceglieranno per volare a NEW YORK per il prestigioso festival della danza? Ancora una volta i ragazzi devono convincere l'importante giuria con il loro pezzo migliore, la contemporary battle e la lezione di danza "tecnica Parson" con la maestra Abby Silva.

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado visita cinque amici ristoratori durante le feste Natalizie, prima tappa" Angelina's" a Staten Island, poi e` la volta di "Marco Polo" e "Luzzo" a Brooklyn. A Manhattan nell'Upper East side, Ornella visita "83 e mezza". Ultima tappa per ujn buon gelato,"Dolce Gelateria" nel Village. (2013)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado continua il suo giro per incontrare il suoi amici ristoratori durante le festività di capodanno. Visitando a midtown Chef Pino alla "La Masseria", chef Stefano e Franco a " Viceversa", a "PizzArte" incontra il pizzaiolo Roberto ed il proprietario Dario, a Queens visita "Ornella Trattoria" dove incontra Vincenzo e "Trattoria l'Incontro"dove incontra lo chef e proprietario Rocco. (2013/2014)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado visita uno dei nuovi trendy ristoranti nel Village in New York City." Rafele" ristorante. Ornella e Chef Raffaele Ronca, prima di iniziare il loro viaggio culinario, fanno shopping al "Green market"a Union Square. Oltre a preparare ricette tipiche Napoletane,lo chef preprara alcune ricette con ingredienti Calabresi e per concludere in dolcezza e Bellezza prepara una sua super specialità la famosa torta di ricotta una delle "Cheese cake' più famose di New York. (2014)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado visita il ristorante Angelina's on the Water per conoscere il nuovo chef Matteo Calciati, oltre a preparare le ricette del nuovo menù del ristorante Ornella vi apre le porte dell' esclusivo Gala annuale che presenta per la NOIAW ( National Organizazion Italian American Woman) dove intervista il simpatico e frizzante Dott. OZ. (2014)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado visita "Amo" ristorante, che si trova nel museo Amo di Verona. Con lo chef Vincenzo Buonocore, Ornella prepara alcune ricette fuori menù, e insieme al sommelier Niccolò Petrilli, scelgono il vino perfetto per ogni piatto. Visita alcune delle cantine più interessanti del territorio ed intervista il vice sindaco della città di Verona Dott. Stefano Casali. (2014)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado visita nuovamente "Luzzo Pizzeria" per fare una chiaccherata con Michele Iuliano. Michele e` un pizzaiolo napoletano proprietario di diversi ristoranti in New York, Michele è anche ideatore di un piccolo forno itinerante. Oltre a preparare delle pizze classiche nel forno tradizionale, Michele prepara delle pizze nel suo nuovo forno su ruote, di fronte al suo ristorante.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita" Via Oreto", un ristorante dal cuore siciliano. Antony prepara le ricette della nonna e della mamma. Accanto la nonna a visionare tutto. In questo episodio Ornella intervsita un esperto del vino siciliano Pietro Cavallo, e parlano dell'importanza di abbinare il giusto vino ad ogni piatto.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita Gente, dove incontra Jay Mitchel un americano appassionato di Italia al punto di aprire un suo ristorante in america per far apprezzare il cibo Italiano ad i suoi clienti.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita il ristorante Barolo, nel cuore di Soho ed incontra il proprietario Paolo Secondo, che oltre a preparare una squsita focaccia Genovese, racconta la sua storia di giovane imprenditore italiano a New York.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage.Ornella Fado, visita il ristorante stellato Cascinale Novo, dove incontra chef Walter Ferreto e preparano delle tipiche ricette Piemontesi. Ornella inoltre visitare la casa del Trifolao di Natale Romagnolo per una caccia al tartufo Bianco.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: In questa puntata, Ornella Fado conduce un favoloso evento dedicato alla Regione Calabria da Eataly Downtown, preparando ricette insieme allo chef calabrese Luca Caligiuro e allo chef italo-americano Ryan Rizzuto.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Ornella Fado è a Milano, all'EXPO, con i giovani della Basilicata del progetto Sano è Lucano. I prodotti tipici lucani ispirano una serie di incontri culinari: dalla barca alla Mondadori, fino a Lady Bu Bistrò e al ristorante stellato Il Luogo di Aimo e Nadia con lo chef Fabio Pisani.Il racconto è arricchito dagli interventi di Sara Farnetti e Pietro Bitonti.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: In questa puntata, Ornella Fado conduce un favoloso evento dedicato alla Regione Calabria da Eataly Downtown, preparando ricette insieme allo chef calabrese Luca Caligiuro e allo chef italo-americano Ryan Rizzuto.

programme.desc: Piero Muscari intervista Alberto Biasi, famoso artista italiano. Alberto racconta delle esperienze della sua infanzia e come queste hanno influenzato le sue opere e la sua arte.

programme.title: Change your Destiny
programme.desc: Continuano le audizioni di Antonio Fini e dei suoi giudici per selezionare i migliori danzatori da invitare al prestigioso festival di danza in NEW YORK. I ragazzi affrontano le tre sfide previste: portare il loro pezzo migliore, la contemporary battle e la lezione di Hip hop con Mirco Giordano e Marcello Gargarelli.

programme.title: Change your Destiny
programme.desc: Terza ed ultima puntata della serie. Antonio Fini ed i suoi giudici sono pronti: chi sceglieranno per volare a NEW YORK per il prestigioso festival della danza? Ancora una volta i ragazzi devono convincere l'importante giuria con il loro pezzo migliore, la contemporary battle e la lezione di danza "tecnica Parson" con la maestra Abby Silva.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: La puntata di oggi è ricca di contenuti. Ornella Fado incontra Cristina Carrani, esperta in materia immobiliare che aiuterà Ornella a scoprire l'Isola di Capri. Dalla moda alla storia, al cibo e all'arte, Brindiamo! Edizione speciale Capri è l'episodio perfetto per festeggiare i 18 anni di Brindiamo! su NYC LIFE.

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado si trova nel laboratorio di Urbani Truffle a New York ed invita i suoi amici chef per preparare insieme delle ricette da gustare durante le festività natalizie. In questa puntata Chef Pino Coladonato, Chef Marcello Russodivito e la giovane pizzaiola Giorgia Caporuscio. In questa edizione di Brindiamo! in my Kitchen ospite speciale la soprano Cristina Fontanelli e John La Barbera, oltre a vari ospiti del mondo dello spettacolo, e della moda. (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado si trova nel laboratorio di Urbani Truffle a New York per la serconda parte di Brindiamo! in my kitchen. I suoi amici chef in questa puntata son lo chef Raffaele Ronca, lo Chef Marco Chirico e il giovane pizzaiolo Ettore Toches. In questa edizione di Brindiamo! in my Kitchen la musica è dal vivo con la soprano Cristina Fontanelli ed il compositore Jhon La Barbera, tra gli ospiti di questa puntata il Fashion designer Domenico Vacca. (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado

(2013/2014)

programme.title: BrindiamoS3
programme.desc: Ornella Fado continua la sua favolsa crociera ospite della MSC crociera, questa volta è diretta a Boston dove visita il ristorante "Pasta Beach" e prepara deliziose ricette con gli chef Andrea Borio, Marco Minieri e Allison Kimmel, sulla nave intervista il Direttore dell'hotel della MSC crociere,Gianfranco Sampiero. Ornella è anche ospite con Sergio Brio del Gala della Juventus che si tiene ogni anno a Boston.

programme.title: BrindiamoS3
programme.desc: Ornella Fado visita la città canadese di Montreal alla scoperta dei ristoranti autentici e italiani di successo. A Montreal conosce Aldo Vincenzo, un uomo d'affari appassionato di cucina, che ha aperto una pizzeria ed un ristorante "Vinizza", molto apprezzati in città. In questa puntata Ornella intervista un suo carissimo amico e grande artista, Massimo Ranieri in tour a New York. (2012)

programme.title: BrindiamoS3
programme.desc: Ornella Fado visita il relais "Villa d'Amelia" nel cuore delle Langhe in Piemonte, dove incontra lo chef stellato Damiano Nigro. Chef Nigro prepara delle ricette tipiche del territorio, e dopo una mattinata in cucina si rilassano e brindano alla vita. (2012)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
